author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XLVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title of the Psalm goeth thus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To the end: for the sons of Korah: a Psalm of David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These sons of Korah have the title also of some other Psalms, and indicate a sweet mystery, insinuate a great Sacrament: wherein let us willingly understand ourselves, and let us acknowledge in the title us who hear, and read, and as in a glass set before us behold who we are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sons of Korah, who are they?…Haply the sons of the Bridegroom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Bridegroom was crucified in the place of Calvary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Recollect the Gospel, where they crucified the Lord, and ye will find Him crucified in the place of Calvary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Furthermore, they who deride His Cross, by devils, as by beasts, are devoured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this also a certain Scripture signified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When God’s Prophet Elisha was going up, children called after him mocking, “Go up thou bald head, Go up thou bald head:” but he, not so much in cruelty as in mystery, made those children to be devoured by bears out of the wood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If those children had not been devoured, would they have lived even till now?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or could they not, being born mortal, have been taken off by a fever?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But so in them had no mystery been shown, whereby posterity might be put in fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none then mock the Cross of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews were possessed by devils, and devoured; for in the place of Calvary, crucifying Christ, and lifting on the Cross, they said as it were with childish sense, not understanding what they said, “Go up, thou bald head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is, “Go up”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Crucify Him, Crucify Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For childhood is set before us to imitate humility, and childhood is set before us to beware of foolishness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To imitate humility, childhood was set before us by the Lord, when He called children to Him, and because they were kept from Him, He said, “Suffer them to come unto Me, for of such is the Kingdom of Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The example of childhood is set before us to beware of foolishness by the Apostle, “Brethren, be not children in understanding:” and again he proposeth it to imitate, “Howbeit in malice be ye children, that in understanding ye may be men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the sons of Korah” the Psalm is sung; for Christians then is it sung.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear it as sons of the Bridegroom, whom senseless children crucified in the place of Calvary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they earned to be devoured by beasts; we to be crowned by Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we acknowledge the humility of our Lord, and of it are not ashamed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are not ashamed of Him called in mystery “the bald” (Calvus), from the place of Calvary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on the very Cross whereon He was insulted, He permitted not our forehead to be bald; for with His own Cross He marked it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, that ye may know that these things are said to us, see what is said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O clap your hands, all ye nations” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were the people of the Jews all the nations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, but blindness in part is happened to Israel, that senseless children might cry, “Calve,” “Calve;” and so the Lord might be crucified in the place of Calvary, that by His Blood shed He might redeem the Gentiles, and that might be fulfilled which saith the Apostle, “Blindness in part is happened unto Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them insult, then, the vain, and foolish, and senseless, and say, “Calve,” “Calve;” but ye redeemed by His Blood which was shed in the place of Calvary, say, “O clap your hands, all ye nations;” because to you hath come down the Grace of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O clap your hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “O clap”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore with the hands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because with good works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not rejoice with the mouth while idle with the hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If ye rejoice, “clap your hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The hands of the nations let Him see, who joys hath deigned to give them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, the hands of the nations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The acts of them doing good works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O clap your hands, all ye nations: shout unto God with the voice of triumph.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both with voice and with hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If with the voice only it is not well, because the hands are slow; if only with the hands it is not well, because the tongue is mute.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Agree together must the hands and tongue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let this confess, these work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shout unto God with the voice of triumph.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord Most High is terrible” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Most High in descending made like one ludicrous, by ascending into Heaven is made terrible.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A great King over all the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only over the Jews; for over them also He is King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of them also the Apostles believed and of them many thousands of men sold their goods, and laid the price at the Apostles’ feet, and in them was fulfilled what in the title of the Cross was written, “The King of the Jews.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is King also of the Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “of the Jews” is little.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O clap your hands, all ye nations: for God is the King of all the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it sufficeth not Him to have under Him one nation: therefore such great price gave He out of His side, as to buy the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath subdued the people under us, and the nations under our feet” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which subdued, and to whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that speak?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely, if Apostles; surely, if Saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For under these God hath subdued the people and the nations, that to-day are they honoured among the nations, who by their own citizens earned to be slain: as their Lord was slain by His citizens, and is honoured among the nations; was crucified by His own, is adored by aliens, but those by a price made His own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For therefore bought He us, that aliens from Him we might not be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thinkest thou then these are the words of Apostles, “He hath subdued the people under us, and the nations under our feet”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Strange that Apostles should speak so proudly, as to rejoice that the nations were put under their feet, that is, Christians under the feet of Apostles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they rejoice that we are with them under the feet of Him who died for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For under Paul’s feet ran they, who would be of Paul, to whom He said, “Was Paul crucified for you?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then here, what are we to understand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath subdued the people under us, and the nations under our feet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All pertaining to Christ’s inheritance are among “all the nations,” and all not pertaining to Christ’s inheritance are among “all the nations:” and ye see so exalted in Christ’s Name is Christ’s Church, that all not yet believing in Christ lie under the feet of Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what numbers now run to the Church; not yet being Christians, they ask aid of the Church; to be succoured by us temporally they are willing, though eternally to reign with us as yet they are unwilling.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When all seek aid of the Church, even they who are not yet in the Church, hath He not “subdued the people under us, and the nations under our feet”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath chosen an inheritance for us, the excellency of Jacob, whom He loved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A certain beauty of Jacob He hath chosen for our inheritance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Esau and Jacob were two brothers; in their mother’s womb both struggled, and by this struggle their mother’s bowels were shaken; and while they two were yet therein, the younger was elected and preferred to the elder, and it was said, “Two peoples are in thy womb, and the elder shall serve the younger.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Among all nations is the elder, among all nations the younger; but the younger is in good Christians, elect, godly, faithful; the elder in the proud, unworthy, sinful, stubborn, defending rather than confessing their sins: as was also the very people of the Jews, “being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and going about to establish their own righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But for that it is said, “The elder shall serve the younger;” it is manifest that under the godly are subdued the ungodly, under the humble are subdued the proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Esau was born first, and Jacob was born last; but he who was last born, was preferred to the first-born, who through gluttony lost his birthright.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So thou hast it written, He longed for the pottage, and his brother said to him, If thou wilt that I give it thee, give me thy birthright.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He loved more that which carnally he desired, than that which spiritually by being born first he had earned: and he laid aside his birthright, that he might eat lentils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But lentils we find to be the food of the Egyptians, for there it abounds in Egypt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence is so magnified the lentil of Alexandria, that it comes even to our country, as if here grew no lentil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore by desiring Egyptian food he lost his birthright.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So also the people of the Jews, of whom it is said, “in their hearts they turned back again into Egypt.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They desired in a manner the lentil, and lost their birthright.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God is gone up with jubilation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even He our God, the Lord Christ, is gone up with jubilation; “the Lord with the sound of a trumpet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Is gone up:” whither, save where we know?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither the Jews followed Him not, even with their eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For exalted on the Cross they mocked Him, ascending into Heaven they did not see Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God hath gone up with jubilation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is jubilation, but admiration of joy which cannot be expressed in words?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As the disciples in joy admired, seeing Him go into Heaven, whom they had mourned dead; truly for the joy, words sufficed not: remained to jubilate what none could express.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was also the voice of the trumpet, the voice of Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is said, “Lift up thy voice like a trumpet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Angels preached the ascension of the Lord: they saw the Disciples, their Lord ascending, tarrying, admiring, confounded, nothing speaking, but in heart jubilant: and now was the sound of the trumpet in the clear voice of the Angels, “Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into Heaven? this is Jesus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if they knew not that it was the same Jesus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Had they not just before seen Him before them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Had they not heard Him speaking with them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, they not only saw the figure of Him present, but handled also His limbs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of themselves then knew they not, that it was the same Jesus?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they being by very admiration, from joy of jubilation, as it were transported in mind, the Angels said, “that same is Jesus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As though they said, If ye believe Him, this is that same Jesus, whom crucified, your feet stumbled, whom dead and buried, ye thought your hope lost.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, this is the same Jesus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath gone up before you, “He shall so come in like manner as ye have seen Him go into Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His Body is removed indeed from your eyes, but God is not separated from your hearts: see Him going up, believe on Him absent, hope for Him coming; but yet through His secret Mercy, feel Him present.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He who ascended into Heaven that He might be removed from your eyes, promised unto you, saying, “Lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly then the Apostle so addressed us, “The Lord is at hand; be careful for nothing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ sitteth above the Heavens; the Heavens are far off, He who there sitteth is near.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing praises to our God, sing praises” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom as Man mocked they, who from God were alienated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing praises to our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is not Man only, but God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Man of the seed of David, God the Lord of David, of the Jews having flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whose” (saith the Apostle) “are the fathers, of whom as concerning the flesh Christ came.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the Jews then is Christ, but according to the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who is this Christ who is of the Jews according to the flesh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who is over all, God blessed for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God before the flesh, God in the flesh, God with the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor only God before the flesh, but God before the earth whence flesh was made; nor only God before the earth whereof flesh was made, but even God before the Heaven which was first made; God before the day which was first made; God before Angels; the same Christ is God: for “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God is the King of all the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And before was He not God of all the earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is He not God of both heaven and earth, since by Him surely were all things made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can say that He is not his God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But not all men acknowledged Him their God; and where He was acknowledged, there only, so to say, He was God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Judah is God known.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not yet was it said to the sons of Korah, “O clap your hands, all ye nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that God known in Judah, is King of all the earth: now by all He is acknowledged, for that is fulfilled which Isaiah saith, “He is thy God who hath delivered thee, the God of the whole earth shall He be called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing ye praises with understanding.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He teacheth us and warneth us to sing praises with understanding, not to seek the sound of the ear, but the light of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Gentiles, whence ye were called that ye might be Christians, adored gods made with hands, and sang praises to them, but not with understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they had sung with understanding, they had not adored stones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When a man sensible sang to a stone insensible, did he sing with understanding?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now, brethren, we see not with our eyes Whom we adore, and yet correctly we adore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Much more is God commended to us, that with our eyes we see Him not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If with our eyes we saw Him, haply we might despise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even Christ seen, the Jews despised; unseen, the Gentiles adored.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God shall reign over all nations” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who reigned over one nation, “shall reign” (saith He) “over all nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When this was said, God reigned over one nation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was a prophecy, the thing was not yet shown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks be to God, we now see fulfilled what before was prophesied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A written promise God sent unto us before the time, the time fulfilled He hath repaid us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God shall reign over all nations,” is a promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God sitteth upon His Holy Seat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then was promised to come, now being fulfilled, is acknowledged and held.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God sitteth upon His Holy Seat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is His Holy Seat?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Haply saith one, The Heavens, and he understandeth well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Christ hath gone up, as we know, with the Body, wherein He was crucified, and sitteth at the right hand of the Father; thence we expect Him to come to judge the quick and the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God sitteth upon His Holy Seat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Heavens are His Holy Seat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou also be His Seat? think not that thou canst not be; prepare for Him a place in thy heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He cometh, and willingly sitteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same Christ is surely “the Power of God, and the Wisdom of God:” and what saith the Scripture of Wisdom Herself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The soul of the righteous is the seat of Wisdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then the soul of the righteous is the seat of Wisdom, be thy soul righteous, and thou shalt be a royal seat of Wisdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And truly, brethren, all men who live well, who act well, converse in godly charity, doth not God sit in them, and Himself command?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy soul obeyeth God sitting in it, and itself commandeth the members.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thy soul commandeth thy members, that so may move the foot, the hand, the eye, the ear, and itself commandeth the members as its servants, but yet itself serveth its Lord sitting within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It cannot well rule its inferior, unless its superior it have not disdained to serve.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The princes of the peoples are gathered together unto the God of Abraham” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: True it is, God said this, and thereupon the Jews prided themselves, and said, “We are Abraham’s children;” priding themselves in their father’s name, carrying his flesh, not holding his faith; by seed cleaving to Him, in manners degenerating.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Lord, what said He to them so priding themselves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If ye are Abraham’s children, do the works of Abraham.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again…“The princes of the peoples:” the princes of the nations: not the princes of one people, but the princes of all people have “gathered together unto the God of Abraham.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of these princes was that Centurion too, of whom but now when the Gospel was read ye heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he was a Centurion having honour and power among men, he was a prince among the princes of the peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ coming to him, he sent his friends to meet Him, nay unto Christ truly passing over to him he sent his friends, and asked that He would heal his servant who was dangerously sick.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when the Lord would come, he sent to Him this message: “I am not worthy that Thou shouldest enter under my roof, but say in a word only, and my servant shall be healed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I also am a man set under authority, having under me soldiers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how he kept his rank! first he mentioned that he was under another, and afterwards that another was under him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am under authority, and I am in authority; both under some I am, and over some I am.…As though he said, If I being set under authority command those who are under me, Thou who art set under no man’s authority, canst not Thou command Thy creature, since all things were made by Thee, and without Thee was nothing made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Say,” then, said he, “in a word, and my servant shall be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I am not worthy that Thou shouldest enter under my roof.”…Admiring at his faith, Jesus reprobates the Jews’ misbelief.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sound to themselves they seemed, whereas they were dangerously sick, when their Physician not knowing they slew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore when He reprobated, and repudiated their pride what said he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I say unto you, that many shall come from the east and west,” not belonging to the kindred of Israel: many shall come to whom He said, “O clap your hands, all ye nations;” “and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Abraham begat them not of his own flesh; yet shall they come and sit down with him in the kingdom of heaven, and be his sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whereby his sons?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not as born of his flesh, but by following his faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But the children of the kingdom,” that is, the Jews, “shall be cast into outer darkness, there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They shall be condemned to outer darkness who are born of the flesh of Abraham, and they shall sit down with him in the kingdom of heaven, who have imitated Abraham’s faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what they who belonged to the God of Abraham?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the mighty gods of the earth are greatly lifted up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who were gods, the people of God, the vineyard of God, whereof it is said, “Judge betwixt Me and My vineyard,” shall go into outer darkness, shall not sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, are not gathered unto the God of Abraham.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the mighty gods of the earth;” they who were mighty gods of the earth, presuming upon earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Themselves; for every man is earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to man was it said, “Dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But man ought to presume upon God, and thence to hope for help, not from himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the earth raineth not upon itself, nor shineth for itself; but as the earth from heaven expecteth rain and light, so man from God ought to expect mercy and truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then, “the mighty gods of the earth, were greatly lifted up,” that is, greatly prided themselves: they thought no physician necessary for themselves, and therefore remained in their sickness, and by their sickness were brought down even to death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The natural branches were broken off that the humble wild olive tree might be grafted in.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hold we fast then, brethren, humility, charity, godliness: since we are called, on their proving reprobate, even by their example let us fear to pride ourselves.
